headline: SBVA Invests €30 Million in Yann LeCun-Founded AMI to Pioneer the Era of World Models
summary: SBVA (CEO: JP Lee) announced today that it has committed a total of €30 million in the seed round of AMI (Advanced Machine Intelligence), a global frontier AI lab founded by Professor Yann LeCun, one of the world's foremost pioneers in artificial intelligence.

content.title: SBVA Invests €30 Million in Yann LeCun-Founded AMI to Pioneer the Era of World Models
content.summary: SBVA (CEO: JP Lee) announced today that it has committed a total of €30 million in the seed round of AMI (Advanced Machine Intelligence), a global frontier AI lab founded by Professor Yann LeCun, one of the world's foremost pioneers in artificial intelligence.

thumbnail.caption: SBVA Invests €30 Million in Yann LeCun–Founded AMI to Pioneer the Era of World Models

headline: The Bull Case For Synaptics (SYNA) Could Change Following New Google-Backed Edge AI Coral Dev Board
summary: In early March 2026, Synaptics announced a limited-edition Coral Dev Board powered by its Astra SL2610 line and Google’s Coral NPU, alongside new Astra SR80 and SRW1500 AI-native microcontrollers aimed at ultra-low-power, always-on Edge AI across audio, IoT, and connected devices. The collaboration with Google Research and the integration of Wi‑Fi 7 and on-device generative AI capabilities highlight Synaptics’ push to make Edge AI development more open, power-efficient, and production-ready...

content.title: The Bull Case For Synaptics (SYNA) Could Change Following New Google-Backed Edge AI Coral Dev Board
content.summary: In early March 2026, Synaptics announced a limited-edition Coral Dev Board powered by its Astra SL2610 line and Google’s Coral NPU, alongside new Astra SR80 and SRW1500 AI-native microcontrollers aimed at ultra-low-power, always-on Edge AI across audio, IoT, and connected devices. The collaboration with Google Research and the integration of Wi‑Fi 7 and on-device generative AI capabilities highlight Synaptics’ push to make Edge AI development more open, power-efficient, and production-ready...

headline: SoftBank's PayPay plans to price US IPO around low end of range, sources say
summary: The initial public offering of SoftBank's PayPay is likely to price around the low end of ‌its marketing range as war in the Middle East roils markets, two people ‌familiar with the matter said.  Tencent, Ant Group's Alipay, and Alphabet's Google have committed to invest in the IPO, one of the people said.  PayPay has played a central role in encouraging Japanese consumers to move away from a preference for cash by offering rebates on its payments app.

content.title: SoftBank's PayPay plans to price US IPO around low end of range, sources say
content.summary: The initial public offering of SoftBank's PayPay is likely to price around the low end of ‌its marketing range as war in the Middle East roils markets, two people ‌familiar with the matter said.  Tencent, Ant Group's Alipay, and Alphabet's Google have committed to invest in the IPO, one of the people said.  PayPay has played a central role in encouraging Japanese consumers to move away from a preference for cash by offering rebates on its payments app.

thumbnail.caption: PayPay app leaflets are displayed at rice dealer's shop in Tokyo

headline: Canal+ taps Google's AI for video production, content recommendation
summary: French media group Canal+ on Wednesday said it had struck a ‌multi-year partnership with Alphabet's Google Cloud to ‌deploy generative artificial intelligence across its production operations and streaming ​platform.  With Netflix spending on AI-driven recommendation engines and Amazon embedding machine learning deep into Prime Video, CANAL+ is betting that Google's firepower can help it ‌punch above its ⁠weight as it races toward a target of up to 100 million subscribers ⁠by 2030 after swallowing South Africa's MultiChoice.

content.title: Canal+ taps Google's AI for video production, content recommendation
content.summary: French media group Canal+ on Wednesday said it had struck a ‌multi-year partnership with Alphabet's Google Cloud to ‌deploy generative artificial intelligence across its production operations and streaming ​platform.  With Netflix spending on AI-driven recommendation engines and Amazon embedding machine learning deep into Prime Video, CANAL+ is betting that Google's firepower can help it ‌punch above its ⁠weight as it races toward a target of up to 100 million subscribers ⁠by 2030 after swallowing South Africa's MultiChoice.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Logo of French TV channel 'Canal Plus' near Paris

headline: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Broadcom’s Q4 Earnings Call
summary: Broadcom’s fourth quarter results saw a positive market reaction, reflecting strong momentum in its AI semiconductor business and improved operating leverage. Management highlighted that demand for custom AI accelerators among its six major customers, including Google and Anthropic, drove a 106% year-on-year increase in AI semiconductor revenue.
